explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 59 from a base nutrition score of 59. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: easy to fit into familiar meals
topPros.explanation: That lowers friction for regular use.

topPros.title: easy protein delivery for normal diets
topPros.explanation: Dairy gets real credit for convenience.

topPros.title: clear calcium identity
topPros.explanation: That is a core category strength.

topCons.title: tolerance varies a lot person to person
topCons.explanation: A category strength is less useful if the food does not agree with you.

topCons.title: processing changes the category a lot
topCons.explanation: The label alone does not tell the whole story.

topCons.title: not everyone tolerates dairy well
topCons.explanation: That is a real use-case limitation.

closing.summary: Plain Yogurt has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: 3.3g of fat. cholesterol, saturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
scenes.subtitleText: 3.3g of fat. cholesterol, saturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

scenes.narrationText: 4.7g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.
scenes.subtitleText: 4.7g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.

scenes.narrationText: 3.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
